title: Monticalia romeroana
taxonomy: Monticalia romeroana is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This classification suggests that Monticalia romeroana shares some common characteristics with other members of the Asteraceae family. The genus Monticalia is further divided into several species, with Monticalia romeroana being one of them. Monticalia romeroana's classification can be broken down into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Angiospermophyta, the class Magnoliopsida, and the order Asterales. Further classification within the family Asteraceae places Monticalia romeroana within the tribe Heliantheae.

intro: Monticalia romeroana, also known as Romero, is a perennial plant species native to the Andean region, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as digestive issues, respiratory problems, and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Monticalia romeroana include phenolic compounds, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Romero is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a variety of ailments, and it is also used in the production of herbal infusions and supplements.
